title: duet agent response
description: The structured response shape that every orchestrator-routed turn produces. Used with `claude --json-schema` and `codex exec --output-schema` on every cross-agent routing call. OpenAI-strict-compliant: every property in `properties` appears in `required`, and optional fields are nullable via `anyOf`. Empirically verified 2026-05-26 against claude-haiku-4-5 and codex gpt-5.5.

response_text.description: The agent's main response. Prose/code/whatever the snippet asked for. This is what gets routed to the next agent, wrapped in the next snippet.

needs_human.description: Non-null when the agent needs a product decision before proceeding. Orchestrator halts the phase and surfaces `reason` + optional `options` to the human at an in-phase exception gate.

reason.description: Why the agent needs a human decision. One sentence.

options.description: Optional list of concrete answers the human can pick. Null if the question is open-ended.

disagree.description: Points the implementer rejects on first principles when responding to a reviewer's critique. Empty array if no disagreements. The orchestrator tracks `point` strings across rounds; if the same point recurs in two consecutive rounds, halt the phase and surface to the human.

point.description: The reviewer's point being disputed, quoted or paraphrased.

reasoning.description: Why the implementer disagrees, from first principles.
